title: Intriguingly Smoky Moroccan Doenjang Couscous Royal
description: A funky and crisp Moroccan dish featuring doenjang, perfect for a sick day, made with low-glycemic-friendly ingredients and served in a fondue pot. This dish combines the flavors of Morocco with the spiciness of doenjang.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, whisk together doenjang, smoked paprika, garlic, and ginger.
Brush the chicken breast with olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Grill the chicken breast for 5-6 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
In a large pot, bring the chicken broth to a boil.
Add the couscous to the pot, cover, and turn off the heat.
Let the couscous steam for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
Slice the grilled chicken breast into thin strips.
Add the doenjang mixture to the pot with the couscous and stir to combine.
Add the sliced chicken to the pot and stir to combine.
